endpoints.name: Used for changing your password
endpoints.description: Pass in your old password and your new password
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Log in to get an API token
endpoints.description: Submit your email and password to get an API token
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Log out
endpoints.description: Log out by deleting your token off the server.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Used for requesting a password reset code
endpoints.description: The admin should run this on behalf of a user who forgot their password.  The API will generate a password reset code which the admin should then provide to the user. The user can use their client to reset their password. Normally the password reset code is mailed to the user, but I didn't want to do this in this case because I didn't want to  introduce the complicated dependency of having an SMTP server just for this purpose. Doing it this way makes it easy for people to adopt this  API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /signin
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Retrieve the information associated with a signin record
endpoints.description: Retrieve the information associated with a signin record
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY. Replace {{SIGNINID}} with a real signinid.

endpoints.name: Update a signin record
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY. Replace {{SIGNINID}} with a real signinid.

endpoints.name: Delete a signin record
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY. Replace {{SIGNINID}} with a real signinid.

endpoints.name: Get signin info
endpoints.description: Returns a list of signin objects sorted by signin ID descending.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a user
endpoints.description: Use this endpoint to create a team member (user) record
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Retrieve the information associated with a team member's user record
endpoints.description: Retrieve the information associated with a user's account
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY. Replace {{USERID}} with a real userid.

endpoints.name: Delete a team member's user record
endpoints.description: To preserve referential integrity in the database, the user account  will not be deleted from the database. Rather, the password will be set to the empty string, effectively preventing that user from logging in. Furthermore, all active sessions for that user will be deleted, as will any password reset tokens.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY. Replace {{USERID}} with a real userid.

endpoints.name: Retrieve the information associated with all team members' user records
endpoints.description: Retrieve the information associated with all team members' user records
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Used for resetting your password when you forgot it
endpoints.description: Another endpoint will generate a password reset code for you. You should  use the client app to submit the reset code along with the new password to change your password.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{C19QRSERVER_API_KEY}} with your C19QRSERVER API KEY.
